data.title: As a developer, I can run the normalize-imports recipe to sort and deduplicate imports
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Normalize-imports recipe for Python and TypeScript — sorts and deduplicates import statements deterministically.
**Why**: Import ordering is the most common mechanical cleanup task. Deterministic normalization eliminates review noise from LLM-generated import ordering variance.
**Scope**:
- IN: normalize-imports recipe for Python and TypeScript, engine adapter per S1's documented interface
- OUT: Ruby normalize-imports, add-parameter (S1/S2), scaffold-route (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a developer can run normalize-imports on a Python file and it sorts and deduplicates imports following PEP 8 / isort conventions
  ← Satisfies: "Initial catalog includes normalize-imports (sort + deduplicate)"
- When this story is complete, a developer can run normalize-imports on a TypeScript file and it sorts and deduplicates imports following the project's convention
  ← Satisfies: "Initial catalog includes normalize-imports (sort + deduplicate)"
- When this story is complete, running normalize-imports twice produces identical output, and 3 consecutive runs produce zero-variance output
  ← Satisfies: "Each recipe is idempotent" and "Determinism: 3 consecutive runs produce zero-variance output"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Must implement against S1's engine adapter interface
- [Testing] Import sorting conventions vary — test against codebases with mixed import styles (relative, absolute, wildcard, re-exports)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
